overview: This four-color Pauper deck leverages cheap flying creatures and efficient instant-speed spells to establish early board presence while maintaining interaction. By recycling key removal and damage spells through Kor Skyfisher, it aims to outpace opponents in the midgame and close out with direct damage or unblockable attackers. The mana base requires careful management to support the wide color requirements without stalling.
win_conditions: Unblockable flying pressure from Glint Hawk and Kor Skyfisher
Direct damage and threat removal via Galvanic Blast
Spell recursion to generate value and outlast opponents
Mana efficiency to maintain tempo across four colors

keep_criteria: Hands with a turn-one Glint Hawk or Galvanic Blast
Kor Skyfisher paired with a recycled spell target
Mana bases that hit at least two of the four colors early
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing untapped entry or fetchable basics), at least one early play (Glint Hawk, Kor Skyfisher, or Galvanic Blast), and a curve that allows a turn-1 or turn-2 spell.
Prioritize keeping hands that can establish board presence or interaction by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Zero lands or three+ lands with no early plays
Hands missing flying creatures or removal
Mana bases heavily skewed to one color
Mulligan to 0 lands, 4+ lands with no early spells, or hands consisting entirely of high-cost threats.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and dropping Glint Hawk for immediate pressure
Use Galvanic Blast to clear early blockers or chip the opponent's life total
Hold interaction for key aggressive threats while establishing board presence
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Glint Hawk or Kor Skyfisher if available.
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast Galvanic Blast to remove a 2-drop, or drop a 2-drop threat.
Sequence lands to ensure colored mana availability
play W/U sources first to enable early spells, then add B/R as needed.
If the format allows fetch lands or shock lands, prioritize them to smooth mana and enable dual-color spells immediately.
mid_game: Deploy Kor Skyfisher to stabilize the board and begin recycling Galvanic Blast
Maintain tempo by trading efficiently and keeping the opponent on the back foot
Manage four-color mana carefully to avoid clashing while casting spells
Treat mana as a tempo resource
hold priority to react to opponent's key spells rather than autopiloting through turns.
Use graveyard strategically if recursion is available, but avoid overcommitting unless necessary.
Prioritize card advantage and efficient 1-for-1 trades that clear the board or protect key threats.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the board is clear.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or heavy targeted removal.
late_game: Recycle high-impact spells repeatedly to exhaust opponent's resources
Push damage with flying creatures while holding Blast for finishers or sweepers
Transition to a value grind if the opponent stabilizes, leveraging recursion
Resilient mid-game threats (Kor Skyfisher variants, larger flyers) combined with targeted removal to clear blockers.
If the format includes a specific win-con, it is typically a combination of board presence, card advantage, and precise removal to close out games in 2-3 turns.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent has no sweepers, and you can close the game in 2-3 turns.
Also commit when you can protect your threats with removal or counters, or when the opponent is at lethal and you have the resources to deliver it.

key_cards.name: Galvanic Blast
key_cards.notes: Use to clear early threats, finish games, or disrupt opponent's key plays. Prioritize holding it up for instant-speed interaction.

key_cards.name: Glint Hawk
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to establish an unblockable attacker. Use haste to push damage immediately or force unfavorable trades.

key_cards.name: Kor Skyfisher
key_cards.notes: Deploy to stabilize and recycle Galvanic Blast or other key spells. Use its trigger strategically to maintain tempo and outvalue opponents.

sequencing_tips: Hold Galvanic Blast up for instant-speed interaction rather than casting it on your turn
Play Glint Hawk early to force blockers before dropping Kor Skyfisher
Recycle Galvanic Blast with Kor Skyfisher only when you have a clear target or need to finish the game
Prioritize mana base consistency by playing lands that support multiple colors early
Trade flying creatures efficiently to preserve board advantage while recycling spells
Common mistake: overcommitting to early threats without protection.
Another: mismanaging mana base in a 4-color deck
always prioritize lands that enter untapped or can be fetched.
Unique interaction: Glint Hawk's flying makes it excellent for trading with sweepers or forcing unfavorable blocks.
Kor Skyfisher's stats make it a solid early blocker.
Galvanic Blast is versatile for both removal and finishing off low-health players.

matchups.advice: Hold Galvanic Blast for key threats like Boros Elite or Lightning Strike. Use Glint Hawk to pressure early and force inefficient trades.
Play around Izzet Charm by keeping Galvanic Blast in hand. Use Kor Skyfisher to recycle spells and outvalue their burn package.
Apply early pressure with Glint Hawk before they stabilize. Recycle Galvanic Blast to clear their threats and close out games.
Maintain tempo with flying creatures and instant-speed removal. Use Kor Skyfisher to disrupt their board development and recycle key spells.

Use cheap, efficient threats that can be cast in response to counterspells. Employ Galvanic Blast to force counterspells into unfavorable trades. Use flashback or alternative costs if available, and prioritize casting spells when the opponent has no mana to respond.

Apply early pressure to disrupt their land drops. Use removal on their ramp targets. Do not let them stabilize; trade efficiently and close the game before their late-game threats. If they go wide, use sweepers or targeted removal to clear the board.

Stabilize with removal and flying creatures. Do not overcommit to board stalls; use direct damage to clear paths. Transition to card advantage once stabilized. Track their hand for removal and hold up interaction to protect your key threats.

Pressure with early threats to force interaction. Use discard to disrupt their hand. Out-value them with card draw and resilient threats. Do not let them control the pace; force trades on your terms and punish their mana base if it is clunky.

Win the resource war. Prioritize card advantage, efficient removal, and protecting your key threats. Track their hand for sweepers. Win by out-resourcing and making better trades. Sideboard in additional interaction and discard to disrupt their game plan.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), targeted removal, and combo win conditions.
Key tells: opponent holding up mana on your turn 3-4, discarding your hand, or playing unusual land drops.
Track their graveyard and library if relevant, and note when they start hoarding mana for late-game threats.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Galvanic Blast, Glint Hawk, Kor Skyfisher

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Glint Hawk, Galvanic Blast, Kor Skyfisher

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Galvanic Blast, Glint Hawk, Kor Skyfisher
